4233.         <member name="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory">
4234.             <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
4235.             <remarks>
4236.             <para>
4237.             The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> class creates <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances that wrap .NET methods
4238.             (specified as <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>). As part of this process, JSON schemas are
4239.             automatically derived for both the function's input parameters (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>)
4240.             and, by default, the function's return type (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>).
4241.             These schemas are produced using the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> and
4242.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>, and enable AI services to understand and
4243.             interact with the function. Return value serialization and schema derivation behavior can be customized
4244.             via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
4245.             respectively.
4246.             </para>
4247.             </remarks>
4248.             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
4249.         </member>
4250.         <member name="F:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory._defaultOptions">
4251.             <summary>Holds the default options instance used when creating function.</summary>
4252.         </member>
4253.         <member name="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory.Create(System.Delegate,Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions)">
4254.             <summary>Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via a delegate.</summary>
4255.             <param name="method">The method to be represented via the created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</param>
4256.             <param name="options">Metadata to use to override defaults inferred from <paramref name="method"/>.</param>
4257.             <returns>The created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
4258.             <remarks>
4259.             <para>
4260.             By default, any parameters to <paramref name="method"/> are sourced from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary
4261.             of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
4262.             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:
4263.             <list type="bullet">
4264.               <item>
4265.                 <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to the <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> passed into
4266.                 the invocation via <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>'s <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameter. The parameter is
4267.                 not included in the generated JSON schema. The behavior of <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters can't be overridden.
4268.               </item>
4269.               <item>
4270.                 By default, <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters are bound from the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> property
4271.                 and are not included in the JSON schema. If the parameter is optional, such that a default value is provided,
4272.                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/> is allowed to be <see langword="null"/>; otherwise, <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments.Services"/>
4273.                 must be non-<see langword="null"/>, or else the invocation will fail with an exception due to the required nature of the parameter.
4274.                 The handling of <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameters can be overridden via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/>.
4275.               </item>
4276.               <item>
4277.                 By default, <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> parameters are bound directly to <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> instance
4278.                 passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> and are not included in the JSON schema. If the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>
4279.                 instance passed to <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/> is <see langword="null"/>, the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> implementation
4280.                 manufactures an empty instance, such that parameters of type <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> can always be satisfied, whether
4281.                 optional or not. The handling of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> parameters can be overridden via
4282.                 <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/>.
4283.               </item>
4284.             </list>
4285.             All other parameter types are, by default, bound from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary passed into <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>
4286.             and are included in the generated JSON schema. This can be overridden by the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ConfigureParameterBinding"/> provided
4287.             via the <paramref name="options"/> parameter; for every parameter, the delegate is enabled to choose if the parameter should be included in the
4288.             generated schema and how its value should be bound, including handling of optionality (by default, required parameters that are not included in the
4289.             <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/> dictionary will result in an exception being thrown). Loosely-typed additional context information can be passed

             <summary>Provides factory methods for creating commonly-used implementations of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>.</summary>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             The <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactory"/> class creates <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instances that wrap .NET methods
             (specified as <see cref="T:System.Delegate"/> or <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/>). As part of this process, JSON schemas are
             automatically derived for both the function's input parameters (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>)
             and, by default, the function's return type (exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>).
             These schemas are produced using the <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.SerializerOptions"/> and
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.JsonSchemaCreateOptions"/>, and enable AI services to understand and
             interact with the function. Return value serialization and schema derivation behavior can be customized
             via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.MarshalResult"/> and <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionFactoryOptions.ExcludeResultSchema"/>,
             respectively.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <related type="Article" href="https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/ai/quickstarts/use-function-calling">Invoke .NET functions using an AI model.</related>
         </member>

             <returns>The created <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> for invoking <paramref name="method"/>.</returns>
             <remarks>
             <para>
             By default, any parameters to <paramref name="method"/> are sourced from the <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments"/>'s dictionary
             of key/value pairs and are represented in the JSON schema for the function, as exposed in the returned <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/>'s
             <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.JsonSchema"/>. There are a few exceptions to this:
             <list type="bullet">
               <item>
                 <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameters are automatically bound to the <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> passed into
                 the invocation via <see cref="M:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction.InvokeAsync(Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionArguments,System.Threading.CancellationToken)"/>'s <see cref="T:System.Threading.CancellationToken"/> parameter. The parameter is

               </item>
               <item>

             point to an instance method on an instance configured to hold the appropriate state. An <see cref="T:System.IServiceProvider"/> parameter can also be
             used to resolve services from a dependency injection container.
             </para>
             <para>
             Return values are serialized to <see cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonElement"/> using <paramref name="serializerOptions"/> if provided,
             or else using <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIJsonUtilities.DefaultOptions"/>. However, return values whose declared type is <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, a
             derived type of <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIContent"/>, or any type assignable from <see cref="T:System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1"/> are not serialized;
             they are returned as-is to facilitate specialized handling.
             </para>
             <para>
             A JSON schema is also derived from the method's return type and exposed via <see cref="P:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunctionDeclaration.ReturnJsonSchema"/>.
             For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Void"/>, <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task"/>, or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask"/>, no return schema is produced.
             For methods returning <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1"/> or <see cref="T:System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1"/>, the schema is derived from the unwrapped result type.
             </para>
             </remarks>
             <exception cref="T:System.ArgumentNullException"><paramref name="method"/> is <see langword="null"/>.</exception>
             <exception cref="T:System.Text.Json.JsonException">A parameter to <paramref name="method"/> is not serializable.</exception>
         </member>

             <summary>
             Creates an <see cref="T:Microsoft.Extensions.AI.AIFunction"/> instance for a method, specified via an <see cref="T:System.Reflection.MethodInfo"/> instance
             and an optional target object if the method is an instance method.

result.content: 1906. 1907. 1908. Provides the base abstraction for all AI agents, defining the core interface for agent interactions and conversation management. 1909. 1910. 1911. serves as the foundational class for implementing AI agents that can participate in conversations 1912. and process user requests. An agent instance may participate in multiple concurrent conversations, and each conversation 1913. may involve multiple agents working together. 1914. 1915. Security considerations: An orchestrates data flow across trust boundaries — 1916. messages are sent to external AI services, context providers, chat history stores, and function tools. Agent Framework 1917. passes messages through as-is without validation or sanitization. Developers must be aware that: 1918. 1919. User-supplied messages may contain prompt injection attempts designed to manipulate LLM behavior. 1920. LLM responses should be treated as untrusted output — they may contain hallucinations, malicious payloads (e.g., scripts, SQL), 1921. or content influenced by indirect prompt injection. Always validate and sanitize LLM output before rendering in HTML, executing as code, 1922. or using in database queries. 1923. Messages with different roles carry different trust levels: system messages have the highest trust and must be developer-controlled; 1924. user, assistant, and tool messages should be treated as untrusted. 1925. 1926. 1927. 1928. 1929. Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile a comprehensive answer:

## Summary of Microsoft.Agents.AI API Surface

Based on exploration of the NuGet packages, here are the answers to your questions:

### 1. **ChatClientAgent Class**

**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Agents.AI`

**Constructors:**
```
ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    string instructions = null,
    string name = null,
    string description = null,
    IList<AITool> tools = null,
    ILoggerFactory loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider services = null)

ChatClientAgent(
    IChatClient chatClient,
    ChatClientAgentOptions options,
    ILoggerFactory loggerFactory = null,
    IServiceProvider services = null)
```

**Key Methods:**
- **RunAsync** - Multiple overloads:
  - `RunAsync(AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `Task<AgentResponse>`
  - `RunAsync(string, AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `Task<AgentResponse>`
  - `RunAsync(ChatMessage, AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `Task<AgentResponse>`
  - `RunAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `Task<AgentResponse>`
  - Generic versions: `RunAsync<T>()` → `Task<AgentResponse<T>>`

- **RunStreamingAsync** - Multiple overloads:
  - `RunStreamingAsync(AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>`
  - `RunStreamingAsync(string, AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>`
  - `RunStreamingAsync(ChatMessage, AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>`
  - `RunStreamingAsync(IEnumerable<ChatMessage>, AgentSession, ChatClientAgentRunOptions, CancellationToken)` → `IAsyncEnumerable<AgentResponseUpdate>`

- `CreateSessionAsync(string conversationId, CancellationToken)` - Create or resume a conversation

**Key Properties:**
- `ChatClient` - The underlying IChatClient
- `Instructions` - System instructions guiding agent behavior
- `Name` - Agent identifier/name
- `Description` - Human-readable description
- `ChatOptions` - Default ChatOptions
- `ChatHistoryProvider` - Optional chat history provider
- `AIContextProviders` - List of context providers

---

### 2. **AgentRunOptions and Maximum Iterations**

**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Agents.AI`

`AgentRunOptions` is the base options class with these properties:
- `ContinuationToken` - For resuming background responses
- `AllowBackgroundResponses` - Enable long-running background operations
- `AdditionalProperties` - Custom metadata dictionary
- `ResponseFormat` - Request structured output (Text, JSON, or JSON schema)

**Note:** `AgentRunOptions` does NOT have a `MaximumIterations` property. Instead:
- **Maximum iterations are controlled in `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.FunctionInvokingChatClient`** via the `MaximumIterationsPerRequest` property (default: 40)
- This controls how many roundtrips can occur during function invocation loops
- The agent framework uses `FunctionInvokingChatClient` internally for tool invocation

`ChatClientAgentRunOptions` extends `AgentRunOptions` with:
- `ChatOptions` - Per-request chat configuration
- `ChatClientFactory` - Factory to replace/decorate the chat client per-request

---

### 3. **Tool Registration Mechanism**

Tools are registered via **`ChatOptions.Tools`**:

```csharp
var chatOptions = new ChatOptions
{
    Tools = new List<AITool> 
    {
        // Tools added here
    }
};
```

Tools can be provided via:
1. **Constructor parameter:** Tools passed to `ChatClientAgent` constructor are augmented with any tools provided via `ChatOptions.Tools` (union behavior)
2. **ChatOptions.Tools** in run options
3. **FunctionInvokingChatClient.AdditionalTools** - For tools the service knows about but aren't sent on each request

---

### 4. **Microsoft.Extensions.AI Package - Tool Creation**

**Namespace:** `Microsoft.Extensions.AI`

**AIFunction:**
- Abstract base class representing an invocable function
- Properties:
  - `UnderlyingMethod` - The MethodInfo being wrapped (if applicable)
  - `JsonSerializerOptions` - For marshaling parameters
- Methods:
  - `InvokeAsync(AIFunctionArguments, CancellationToken)` → Returns function result
  - `AsDeclarationOnly()` → Creates non-invocable declaration

**AIFunctionFactory:**
Static factory methods to create AIFunction instances from delegates or MethodInfo:
```csharp
// From delegate
AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    Delegate method,
    AIFunctionFactoryOptions options = null)

AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    Delegate method,
    string name,
    string description,
    JsonSerializerOptions serializerOptions)

// From MethodInfo
AIFunctionFactory.Create(
    MethodInfo method,
    object target,
    AIFunctionFactoryOptions options)
```

**AIFunctionFactoryOptions:**
- `SerializerOptions` - Custom JSON serialization
- `JsonSchemaCreateOptions` - Schema generation behavior
- `MarshalResult` - Custom result serialization
- `ExcludeResultSchema` - Skip return type schema generation
- `ConfigureParameterBinding` - Custom parameter binding logic

**ChatOptions.Tools:**
```csharp
var chatOptions = new ChatOptions 
{
    Tools = new[] 
    {
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(() => MyMethod()),
        AIFunctionFactory.Create(MyDelegate)
    }
};
```

---

### 5. **ChatClientAgent Construction - IChatClient Requirement**

**Yes, ChatClientAgent takes an IChatClient as the first required parameter.**

```csharp
var chatClient = /* IChatClient instance */;
var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, instructions: "...");
```

**Other parameters:**

| Parameter | Type | Purpose |
|-----------|------|---------|
| `chatClient` | `IChatClient` | **Required** - The underlying chat client |
| `instructions` | `string?` | System instructions for agent behavior |
| `name` | `string?` | Agent identifier/display name |
| `description` | `string?` | Agent description for discovery |
| `tools` | `IList<AITool>?` | Pre-registered tools (augmented with ChatOptions.Tools) |
| `loggerFactory` | `ILoggerFactory?` | For logging |
| `services` | `IServiceProvider?` | DI container for tool resolution |

Or use the options constructor:
```csharp
var options = new ChatClientAgentOptions 
{
    Name = "ResearchAgent",
    ChatOptions = new ChatOptions { Tools = myTools },
    ChatHistoryProvider = myHistoryProvider,
    UseProvidedChatClientAsIs = false,
    RequirePerServiceCallChatHistoryPersistence = true
};

var agent = new ChatClientAgent(chatClient, options, loggerFactory, services);
```

---

**Key Location Summary:**
- Main agent API: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai/1.0.0/lib/net9.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.xml`
- Base abstractions: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.agents.ai.abstractions/1.0.0/lib/net9.0/Microsoft.Agents.AI.Abstractions.xml`
- Tool/function APIs: `/home/runner/.nuget/packages/microsoft.extensions.ai.abstractions/10.4.1/lib/net9.0/Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions.xml`
